note: workflows-lite — universal subset for non-guild projects. GENERATED from workflows.json by guild/gen_workflows_lite.py — do not hand-edit.

workflows.name: Standard Mission
workflows.tagline: The full guild pipeline for serious, multi-step missions.
workflows.when: Use this when the work is large, multi-disciplinary, or needs several specialists coordinated in waves — the guild's conquering-campaign, planned and driven to completion.

steps.title: Place the Order
steps.act: You describe the mission in plain language so the guild knows what to build.

steps.label: You review and approve the brief before the guild begins planning.

steps.title: Form the Campaign Plan
steps.act: Runs `guild/plan.py` — the Strategist's planning step as a runnable primitive.

steps.title: Design the Interface
steps.act: The Designer shapes the visual design and UI for any user-facing part of the mission, so the build has a clear visual target to hit. The guild's only designer — no one else touches the look.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the plan and design are buildable, complete, and safe to start construction.

steps.title: Implement the Plan
steps.act: The Developer writes the code, fixes any bugs, and self-tests everything before handing it back.

steps.title: Clean Up and Verify
steps.act: The Quartermaster runs final cleanup checks and confirms the whole mission works end to end.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the finished mission — what was built and any follow-ups — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Quick Fix
workflows.tagline: A lean path for small, well-understood changes.
workflows.when: Use this for small bugs, UI tweaks, or a pasted error to fix (and any of the same class) where planning would only slow things down.

steps.title: Report the Bug
steps.act: You flag a small, well-understood bug or change for the guild to fix.

steps.label: You confirm the fix scope is correct before the Developer starts work.

steps.title: Make the Fix
steps.act: The Developer applies the smallest correct change and tests it locally before handing it off.

steps.title: Verify the Fix
steps.act: The Quartermaster double-checks the change and confirms nothing else broke in the process.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the fix and its verification — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Design Sprint
workflows.tagline: From visual concept to shipped UI in one tight sprint.
workflows.when: Use this when the work centers on UI, UX, brand, or visual quality — including admin/members panel layout redesigns and card/panel consolidation, the most frequent visual work on the app.

steps.title: Request the Sprint
steps.act: You describe the UI or UX problem you want the guild to design around.

steps.label: You approve the design brief before the sprint officially kicks off.

steps.title: Plan the Sprint
steps.act: Runs `guild/plan.py` — the Strategist's planning step as a runnable primitive.

steps.title: Design the Visuals
steps.act: The Designer produces the UI/UX mockups, components, and brand treatments for the sprint.

steps.title: Build the Design
steps.act: The Developer implements the design faithfully in code, respecting the visual spec.

steps.title: Clean Up and Verify
steps.act: The Quartermaster polishes the build and verifies the design holds up in real interactions.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the design-sprint outcome, with screenshots and notes — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Architecture Build
workflows.tagline: Schemas, systems, and structural change, done right.
workflows.when: Use this when the work touches the database, system design, or core architecture — including performance tuning and finance-critical data migrations (plan, dry-run with BEGIN/ROLLBACK, reconcile).

steps.title: Request the Build
steps.act: You describe the system, schema, or architectural change you need the guild to make.

steps.label: You approve the architecture brief before scoping and structural work begin.

steps.title: Scope the Work
steps.act: Runs `guild/plan.py` — the Strategist's planning step as a runnable primitive.

steps.title: Architect the System
steps.act: The Architect drafts the system architecture and database schema to satisfy the scope.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the architecture is sound, consistent, and safe to implement.

steps.title: Build Migrations and Code
steps.act: The Developer writes the schema migrations and implements the system against the certified design.

steps.title: Integrate the Modules
steps.act: The Developer assembles the slice diffs, reconciles seams against the shared contract, runs the build, and relies on the inline verify-gate Critic to review the aggregate diff before a single commit.

steps.title: Clean Up and Verify
steps.act: The Quartermaster runs final checks and confirms the architecture works as intended in production-like conditions.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the architecture build, its verification, and any caveats — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Bug Cycle
workflows.tagline: Pull a reported bug, hunt the real root cause, fix it, and close the loop.
workflows.when: Use this for bugs filed in the bug_reports table — the daily triage-and-fix loop. The recurring Lex signatures that belong here: a missing i18n message key rendering raw in the UI, a missing DB column, or an RLS / permission-denied error on a view or RPC.

steps.title: Report or Pull the Bug
steps.act: You file a bug, or ask the guild to pull the open rows from the bug_reports table.

steps.label: You confirm the bug and its scope before the hunt begins.

steps.title: Triage & Reproduce
steps.act: The Strategist pulls the row, reads primary_instructions, and reproduces the report to find the true root cause — not just the symptom.

steps.title: Fix at the Root
steps.act: The Developer applies the smallest correct fix following project conventions, and self-tests it locally.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the fix genuinely resolves the bug — DB fixes count now; frontend fixes only after deploy — before the status is flipped.

steps.title: Close & File Spawn
steps.act: The Developer flips the bug_reports row to Fixed and files any new bugs surfaced during the hunt back into the table.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the bug, its root cause, the fix, and any new bugs filed — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Security Sweep
workflows.tagline: Audit the database security surface and seal every leak — propose first, apply on sign-off.
workflows.when: Use this for RLS policies, SECURITY DEFINER RPCs, anon/authenticated grants, and advisor-driven security audits.

steps.title: Call the Sweep
steps.act: You ask the guild to audit the app's security surface for leaks and over-broad access.

steps.label: You confirm the read-only boundary before the audit begins.

steps.title: Scope the Surface
steps.act: Runs `guild/plan.py` — the Strategist's planning step as a runnable primitive.

steps.title: Hunt the Holes
steps.act: The Architect audits the security surface across the OWASP Top-10 lens: pulls Supabase advisors and maps every leak, missing RLS policy, and over-broad grant (A01); scans for hardcoded secrets/credentials (A02), injection and unvalidated input (A03), and outdated/vulnerable dependencies via npm audit (A06).

steps.label: You sign off on each proposed change before any security fix is applied — nothing is written without explicit approval.

steps.title: Seal the Breaches
steps.act: The Developer applies the approved RLS/policy/grant fixes and re-proves each gate holds.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports what was audited, what was sealed, and any risks deferred — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Comms Triage
workflows.tagline: Sweep email, calendar, and WhatsApp into tasks, events, and replies.
workflows.when: Use this to triage your inbox and messages — turn what matters into tasks and calendar entries, and prep for meetings.

steps.title: Call the Triage
steps.act: You ask the guild to go through your email, calendar, and WhatsApp and surface what needs you.

steps.label: You confirm the scope before the Butler drafts anything or creates events.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports what needs you, what was scheduled, and the drafts awaiting your send — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Inquiry / Recon
workflows.tagline: Answer a read-only question about the repo or guild — cited to file:line.
workflows.when: Use this for a quick read-only question about the code, the repo, or the guild itself — 'where is X', 'what calls Y', 'how does Z work', 'find all uses of W', 'which file holds …'. The routed member scouts with read-only tools (grep/glob/read), verifies findings against source, and returns a file:line-cited answer. No code is written, nothing is mutated. This is the lightweight lane so trivial lookups have a home under the workflow-gate hard rule, without the weight of a Strategic Audit.

steps.title: Pose the Question
steps.act: You ask a read-only question about the code, the repo, or the guild.

steps.title: Scout & Answer
steps.act: The routed member scouts read-only — grep/glob/read across the repo (handing bulk reads/summaries to a doer weapon) — verifies every claim against source, and returns a concise answer cited to file:line. Writes nothing.

steps.label: The Butler reports the answer and its file:line sources back to you in plain English, and flags whether the run is worth saving as a reusable workflow.

workflows.name: Conversation
workflows.tagline: Plain talk — a greeting, an ack, or a meta-question with no specialist lane.
workflows.when: The catch-all lane for any turn that fits no other workflow: greetings, acknowledgements, thanks, clarifications, and meta-questions about the guild itself. It exists so EVERY turn — even one that fires no tools and writes nothing — can declare a workflow under the turn-end banner gate. The Butler answers directly in plain English; nothing is mutated. If a turn turns out to need real work, route it to the proper specialist lane instead of staying here.

steps.title: Say the Word
steps.act: You greet, acknowledge, or ask a meta-question that fits no specialist lane.

steps.label: The Butler's plain-English reply IS the report back to you; he flags if the turn should have been a real workflow instead.
